summary: Mild maxillary incisor spacing in children under 12 is typically a normal variant due to ongoing growth and developmental changes. Observation remains the recommended initial approach unless there are signs suggesting an anatomical abnormality such as microdontia or supernumerary teeth warranting diagnostic imaging.
